reason: Core source compiles and runs through the shipped CLI.
Generics are covered by the current regression matrix.
Package-root checking and locked imports are currently shipped.
The built-in test path is part of the current alpha gate.
Pretty diagnostics and exit code 1 are regression-tested.
Await outside an async context emits stable E-ASYNC-CONTEXT metadata, operation, guidance, and source span.
Multiple diagnostics and source context are covered.
The embedded runtime links and executes the basic native program.
GC roots now cover suspended task-frame data with forced-GC ASAN/UBSAN evidence; broader RFC runtime ownership and scheduler integration remain incomplete.
Exception payload cleanup is exercised; the complete runtime exception contract is not done.
Native corpus now executes a no-await async function through a spawned frame, awaits its result, and completes join successfully.

Native corpus covers bounded suspension/resumption and cooperative cancellation; the runtime now exposes borrowed repeated-join outcome observations, while Aura-level failure/cancellation propagation and payload handling remain partial.

Bounded straight-line, branch-join, multi-await, and one top-level Int loop/await shape use frame lowering; general control flow, richer ownership, and full outcome propagation remain outside the broader contract.
Process argument forwarding and std.io args are shipped.

Scheduler-integrated typed file/TCP operation completion, cancellation cleanup, and Aura/native smoke are shipped; compiler-generated arbitrary async I/O, peer-failure matrices, and full backpressure remain open.

A runnable Aura-level primitive HTTP/TCP fixture now covers request status, response serialization, and loopback; typed handles, async handlers, keep-alive suspension, and response backpressure remain open.
Default output is a single executable.
Host-only validation proves exact required-target/package/workflow wiring and rejects policy-only targets; foreign compilation and execution still require declared CI hosts.
Package graph resolution and checking are currently exercised.
Locked dependency input is supported without requiring a network call.

Offline acceptance now verifies publish receipts, update checksums, rollback, and aura-sig-v1 trusted-key/tamper/replay evidence; live registry compatibility, production artifact signing, and credentialed network acceptance remain open.
The runtime defines primitive, TCP-header, owned-value, opaque-handle, callback, and bounded std.net FFI surfaces; typed foreign handles across Aura async boundaries remain open.

Bounded owned values, TCP headers, opaque handles, callbacks, std.net primitive HTTP/loopback, ABI mismatch, linker, and sanitizer fixtures are shipped; compiler-level typed-handle async-boundary proof remains open.
AddressSanitizer and UBSan smoke coverage is wired into CI.

Deterministic native sanitizer seeds now fail closed under ASAN/UBSAN/LSAN; the generated Array/String ownership fixture also passes forced leak detection, while the broader Aura-generated matrix remains ASAN/UBSAN-only because exception payload ownership still leaks, so end-to-end boundary coverage remains partial.
Archives, deterministic metadata, and per-archive SHA-256 checksums are produced.
Local package/install and CLI smoke paths are available.
Host-only contract validation and native/cross-file acceptance wiring are verified; Linux arm64/Windows native package and execution evidence still require external hosts.
Release workflow now wires deterministic manifests and fail-closed mandatory minisign verification; local cryptographic signing and credentialed production execution remain unavailable.
